---
title: "Early Warning System for Harmful Narratives and Radicalisation in Kenya"
subtitle: "AI-Driven Decision Support Platform — Concept Note"
author: "Noah Wamalwa"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: false
    number_sections: false
    latex_engine: xelatex
geometry: "margin=1in"
fontsize: 11pt
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{fancyhdr}
  - \usepackage{graphicx}
  - \usepackage{xcolor}
  - \usepackage{mdframed}
  - \definecolor{ncicblue}{HTML}{0066CC}
  - \definecolor{ncicred}{HTML}{DC3545}
  - \definecolor{warningbg}{HTML}{FFF8E1}
  - \pagestyle{fancy}
  - \fancyhf{}
  - \fancyhead[L]{\small \textcolor{ncicblue}{\textbf{Radicalisation Signals}} \textcolor{gray}{| NCIC Cap 170 Intelligence Platform}}
  - \fancyhead[R]{\small \textcolor{gray}{RESTRICTED — Not for Public Distribution}}
  - \fancyfoot[C]{\small \thepage}
  - \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0.4pt}
---

\vspace{-1em}

\begin{mdframed}[backgroundcolor=warningbg, linecolor=orange, linewidth=1pt, innertopmargin=8pt, innerbottommargin=8pt]
\small \textbf{⚠ Important:} AI classifications are analytical aids, not legal findings. This platform is a decision-support tool for trained NCIC intelligence officers. All Level 4–5 classifications require independent officer validation and legal review before any action is taken. \textbf{This document is restricted and not for public distribution.}
\end{mdframed}

\vspace{1em}

# Overview

**Radicalisation Signals** is an AI-assisted intelligence platform designed to support the **National Cohesion and Integration Commission (NCIC)** of Kenya in monitoring and analysing online content linked to hate speech, ethnic incitement, dehumanisation, and radicalisation.

The platform enables **early detection of harmful narratives** across digital platforms — supporting timely, coordinated, and evidence-based interventions at both national and sub-county levels. It is built on the NCIC Act **Cap 170 framework** and integrates human expert validation at every stage of the classification pipeline.

---

# What the System Does

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| **Signal ingestion** | Monitors Twitter/X, Facebook, TikTok, Telegram, and WhatsApp |
| **AI classification** | Classifies content against the NCIC 6-level taxonomy using GPT-4o-mini |
| **Risk scoring** | Composite risk formula incorporating AI confidence, keywords, network exposure, and source history |
| **Officer validation** | Human-in-the-loop review with override, escalation, and confirmation workflows |
| **Forecasting** | 14-day county-level escalation projections based on signal trends and velocity |
| **Reporting** | Automated Excel exports: Quick Briefing, Detailed Report, NCIC Legal Report, County Report |
| **Dashboard** | Interactive map, trend charts, network analysis, and geographic hotspot visualisation |

Platform: [https://wnwamalwa.shinyapps.io/Radicalisation-Signals/](https://wnwamalwa.shinyapps.io/Radicalisation-Signals/)

---

# Data Coverage

The platform provides comprehensive, multi-dimensional monitoring across Kenya's digital information environment.

**Geographic Scope**

All **47 counties** of Kenya are covered using real **IEBC administrative boundaries**, enabling precise geospatial analysis at the county and sub-county (ward) level. Counties are mapped to known sub-locations — for example, Nairobi signals are attributed to specific areas including Kibera, Kayole, Mathare, Korogocho, Eastleigh, Kasarani, and Embakasi, enabling targeted community-level interventions.

**Monitoring Window**

The platform operates on a **rolling 30-day window**. All historical cases are automatically re-scored whenever an intelligence officer refines the keyword weights through validation — ensuring that new insights from recent cases are applied retroactively across the entire dataset, not just future classifications.

**Platform Coverage**

| Platform | Relevance |
|---|---|
| **Twitter/X** | High-velocity public discourse; political commentary and incitement often breaks here first |
| **Facebook** | Dominant platform for community groups, electoral mobilisation, and ethnic narrative spread |
| **TikTok** | Emerging vector for youth-targeted radicalisation content; short-form video with high reach |
| **Telegram** | Encrypted channels used for coordinated content distribution and closed community organising |
| **WhatsApp** | Primary mass communication channel in Kenya; key vector for misinformation and forward chains |

**Language Coverage**

| Language | Context |
|---|---|
| **Swahili** | National language; most monitored content is in Swahili or Swahili-English code-switching |
| **English** | Official language; formal political and media discourse |
| **Sheng** | Urban youth vernacular (Nairobi, Mombasa, Kisumu); frequently used to evade keyword detection |
| **Kikuyu** | Central Kenya; high relevance during electoral periods in Mt Kenya region |
| **Luo** | Nyanza region; significant during inter-community tension events |
| **Kalenjin** | Rift Valley; historically associated with PEV-era rhetoric patterns |

**Note on language accuracy:** Classification performance is highest for Swahili and English. Mixed-language (code-switched) posts, particularly in Sheng, may have lower accuracy. Content in Kikuyu, Luo, or Kalenjin without Swahili/English elements is the most challenging for the current model.

**Geospatial Infrastructure**

County polygon boundaries are derived from the **official IEBC shapefile**, providing legally authoritative administrative boundaries for all 47 counties. The choropleth map uses a green-to-red risk gradient with explicit grey rendering for counties with insufficient data, ensuring officers are never misled by empty polygons.

---

# The NCIC 6-Level Classification Taxonomy

All content is classified against Cap 170 on a six-point scale:

| Level | Label | Action |
|---|---|---|
| **L0** | Neutral Discussion | No action. Log for trend analysis. |
| **L1** | Offensive Language | Monitor. Flag for weekly review. |
| **L2** | Prejudice / Stereotyping | Document pattern. Request platform review if repeated. |
| **L3** | Dehumanization | Issue monitoring flag. Initiate engagement with poster. |
| **L4** | Hate Speech | **Section 13 threshold met.** Require takedown. Alert DCI. |
| **L5** | Toxic | **IMMEDIATE ESCALATION.** Press charges. Contact DCI now. |

Levels 0–1 are protected speech. Levels 4–5 cross the Section 13 legal threshold.

---

# Classification Methodology

The platform applies a three-stage decision chain to every post before assigning an NCIC level:

**Stage 1 — Violence Override (applied unconditionally first)**
Any explicit or implicit call to kill, attack, burn, or physically harm — regardless of the target — immediately classifies the post L4 or L5. The activism test is blocked. Examples: *'Kill the MP'* → L5. *'Mpige risasi'* → L5.

**Stage 2 — Target Test**
If violence is absent: is the target a *protected group* (ethnic, religious, or regional community) or a *public official/institution*? Criticism of officials defaults to L0/L1 — political accountability is constitutionally protected. Targeting a protected group may qualify L2–L5.

**Stage 3 — Intent Test**
What outcome does the speaker seek? Accountability ('vote out', 'resign', 'audit') → L0/L1. Exclusion ('waende', 'go back') → L3/L4. Dehumanisation ('they are vermin') → L3+. Mobilisation against a group → L4.

**Risk Formula:**
$$\text{Risk} = 0.55 \times \text{composite} + 0.45 \times \text{NCIC\_base}$$
$$\text{composite} = 0.30 \times \text{confidence} + 0.22 \times \text{keyword} + 0.13 \times \text{network} + 0.08 \times \text{spike} + 0.12 \times \text{context} + 0.15 \times \text{source\_history}$$

---

# Safeguards and Responsible Use

The platform incorporates multiple layers of protection against misclassification and misuse:

1. **Mandatory human review** — No legal or enforcement action is triggered by AI alone. Every L2+ case requires an NCIC officer to Confirm, Downgrade, Escalate, or Clear.
2. **Activism protection** — The three-stage chain explicitly protects political accountability speech from being misclassified as hate speech.
3. **Continuous re-calibration** — Officer validations update keyword weights in real time. All historical cases are automatically re-scored.
4. **Deterministic classification** — temperature=0 ensures consistent results; responses are cached by content hash.
5. **Unit-tested logic** — 24 automated tests cover risk scoring, source context, violence override, and edge cases.
6. **Cross-session synchronisation** — All officer decisions are persisted to SQLite and synchronised across concurrent sessions every 5 seconds.

---

# Target Users

| User Group | Use |
|---|---|
| NCIC Intelligence Officers | Primary users — validate, escalate, and act on classified content |
| Government Analysts & Policy Units | Strategic oversight and briefing |
| Security and Intelligence Agencies | Operational coordination and escalation |
| Researchers and CSOs | Evidence-based advocacy and analysis |

**Note:** This is a restricted, non-public system. Unauthorised access or distribution of outputs is prohibited.

---

# Institutional Framework

**Lead Agency:** National Cohesion and Integration Commission (NCIC)

**Supporting Institutions (Proposed):**

- National Intelligence Service (NIS)
- Anti-Terrorism Police Unit (ATPU)
- Communications Authority of Kenya (CA)
- Media Council of Kenya (MCK)
- National Intelligence and Research University (NIRU)
- United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)

---

# Expected Outcomes

- Early detection of harmful narratives and radicalisation risks before they escalate
- Identification of county and sub-county level hotspots for targeted interventions
- Improved inter-agency coordination with a shared, evidence-based intelligence picture
- Data-driven policy formulation and operational decision-making
- Strengthened national cohesion and conflict prevention capacity

---

# Value Proposition

This platform enables Kenya to shift from **reactive response** to **proactive, geographically targeted prevention** — using AI to support coordinated national action within a legal, human-rights-compliant framework anchored in Cap 170.

\vspace{2em}

---

\begin{center}
\small \textcolor{gray}{
\textbf{Noah Wamalwa} · Project Lead / Developer \\
Radicalisation Signals v5 · NCIC Cap 170 · GPT-4o-mini + Human-in-the-Loop · Kenya \\
\textit{Developed for the IEA Kenya NIRU AI Hackathon} \\
\href{https://wnwamalwa.shinyapps.io/Radicalisation-Signals/}{https://wnwamalwa.shinyapps.io/Radicalisation-Signals/}
}
\end{center}
